text: Lesson 57
zh: 第57课

text: Back in the old country
zh: 回到故乡

text: Did the narrater find his mother's grave?
zh: 叙述者找到他母亲的坟墓了吗？

text: I stopped to let the car cool off and to study the map.
zh: 我停下车，让引擎冷却一下，同时看看地图。

text: I had expected to be near my objective by now, but everything still seemed alien to me.
zh: 我原以为这时应该快到目的地了，但眼前的一切对我来说仍然十分陌生。

text: I was only five when my father had taken me abroad, and that was eighteen years ago.
zh: 父亲带我出国时我才五岁，那已经是十八年前的事了。

text: When my mother had died after a tragic accident, he did not quickly recover from the shock and loneliness.
zh: 母亲因一场悲惨的事故去世后，他久久未能从悲痛和孤独中恢复过来。

text: Everything around him was full of her presence, continually reopening the wound.
zh: 他身边的一切都让他想起她的音容笑貌，不断揭开心中的伤痛。

text: So he decided to emigrate.
zh: 于是他决定移居国外。

text: In the new country he became absorbed in making a new life for the two of us, so that he gradually ceased to grieve.
zh: 在新的国家，他一心扑在为我们俩开创新生活上，渐渐地也就不再那么悲伤了。

text: He did not marry again and I was brought up without a woman's care; but I lacked for nothing, for he was both father and mother to me.
zh: 他没有再娶，我也是在没有女性照料下长大的；但我什么都不缺，因为他一人身兼父母之职。

text: He always meant to go back one day, but not to stay.
zh: 他一直打算有朝一日回去看看，但并不打算久留。

text: His roots and mine had become too firmly embedded in the new land.
zh: 他和我的根都已经深深扎在了这片新的土地上。

text: But he wanted to see the old folk againand to visit my mother's grave.
zh: 但他想再见见故乡的老人，也想去看看我母亲的坟墓。

text: He became mortally ill a few months before we had planned to go and, when he knew that he was dying, he made me promise to go on my own.
zh: 就在我们计划动身前几个月，他患上了不治之症；他知道自己将不久于人世时，让我答应一个人去完成这个心愿。

text: I hired a car the day after landing and bought a comprehensive book of maps,
zh: 我一到达就租了辆车，还买了一本详尽的地图册，

text: which I found most helpful on the cross-country journey, but which I did not think I should need on the last stage.
zh: 这本书在跨国旅程中帮了我很大的忙，但我以为最后一段路程用不着它了。

text: It was not that I actually remembered anything at all.
zh: 倒不是说我确实还记得什么。

text: But my father had described over and over again what we should see at every milestone after leaving the nearest town,
zh: 只是父亲一遍又一遍地给我描述过，从最近的那座城镇出发后，每隔一段里程会看到什么样的景象，

text: so that I was positive I should recognize it as familiar territory.
zh: 所以我坚信自己一眼就能认出那是熟悉的地方。

text: Well, I had been wrong, for I was now lost.
zh: 然而，我错了，因为这时我迷路了。

text: I looked at the map and then at the milometer.
zh: 我看看地图，又看看里程表。

text: I had come ten miles since leaving the town and at this point, according to my father,
zh: 从城镇出发以来，我已经走了十英里，照父亲的说法，在这一点上，

text: I should be looking at farms and cottages in a valley,
zh: 我应该能看到山谷里的农场和农舍，

text: with the spire of the church of our village showing in the far distance.
zh: 远处还能看到我们村教堂的尖顶。

text: I could see no valley, no farms, no cottages and no church spire-only a lake.
zh: 可我看不到什么山谷，没有农场，没有农舍，也没有教堂尖顶——只有一片湖。

text: I decided that I must have taken a wrong turning somewhere.
zh: 我断定自己一定是在什么地方拐错了弯。

text: So I drove back to the town and began to retrace the route, taking frequent glances at the map.
zh: 于是我把车开回城镇，一边频频查看地图，一边重新走一遍原路。

text: I landed up at the same corner.
zh: 结果我又到了同一个地方。

text: The curious thing was that the lake was not marked on the map.
zh: 奇怪的是，这片湖在地图上根本没有标出来。

text: I felt as if I had stumbled into a nightmare country, as you sometimes do in dreams.
zh: 我觉得自己仿佛闯入了一个噩梦般的国度，就像人有时在梦里经历的那样。

text: And, as in a nightmare, there was nobody in sight to help me.
zh: 而且，正如噩梦中一样，四下里没有一个人可以求助。

text: Fortunately for me, as I was wondering what to do next,
zh: 幸运的是，就在我正犯愁下一步该怎么办的时候，

text: there appeared on the horizon a man on horseback, riding in my direction.
zh: 地平线上出现了一个骑马的人，正朝我这边走来。

text: I waited till he came near, then I asked him the way to our old village.
zh: 我等他走近，便向他打听去我们那个老村子的路。

text: He said that there was now no village.
zh: 他说现在已经没有那个村子了。

text: I thought he must have misunderstood me, so I repeated its name.
zh: 我想他一定是听错了，于是又把村名重复了一遍。

text: This time he pointed to the lake.
zh: 这一次，他指了指那片湖。

text: The village no longer existed because it had been submerged, and all the valley too.
zh: 那个村子已经不复存在，因为它连同整个山谷都被淹没了。

text: The lake was not a natural one, but a man-made reservoir.
zh: 那片湖并非天然形成，而是一座人工水库。
